results.title: [Literature Review] WeaveBench: A Long-Horizon, Real ...
results.content:
WeaveBench is a novel, long-horizon, real-world benchmark designed to evaluate computer-use agents (CUAs) operating with hybrid interfaces, specifically visual desktop control (GUI) and command-line/code execution (CLI/code). Existing benchmarks often assess these interfaces in isolation, failing to test an agent's ability to orchestrate complex workflows that necessitate interleaved operations across both modalities. WeaveBench addresses this critical gap by providing 114 tasks spanning 8 real-world work domains, each requiring agents to seamlessly combine GUI observations/actions with CLI/code operations within a single execution trajectory. Tasks are grounded in genuine user requests and publicly verifiable artifacts. [...] The benchmark's core methodology is built upon rigorous task admission criteria and a sophisticated evaluation protocol.
Task Admission Criteria: A WeaveBench task must satisfy three properties:

Task Construction: Tasks are built through a four-stage pipeline:

Evaluation Protocol: Trajectory-Aware Agentic Judge:
Traditional outcome-only grading is insufficient for hybrid rollouts, as final deliverables can be synthesized, metrics hard-coded, or states reached through specification-violating tool use. WeaveBench employs a trajectory-aware agentic judge (an isolated OpenClaw agent, fixed at GPT-5.5) that re-fetches evidence over multiple turns using file, image, and shell tools.
The scoring follows a five-layer pipeline: [...] `screenshot`
`pyautogui`
`click`
`type`

Main Results:

`exec: shell`
`exec`

Failure Mechanism Analysis:
Analysis of 1,735 failures from frontier backbones reveals that 65.6% of failures fall into two hybrid-specific families:

Conclusion: WeaveBench exposes a critical gap in CUA evaluation by requiring sustained orchestration across visual and programmatic operations. Its design implications recommend coupling presence checks with provenance evidence, making honest abstention a scorable state, grading semantic conformance and tool selection, and auditing process traces to catch CLI-bypass.

results.title: WEAVE: Unleashing and Benchmarking the Interleaved Cross ...
results.content:
WeaveBench evaluates 4 core metrics:

| Metric | Code | Description | Requires Text | Requires Image |
 ---  --- 
| Key Point Correctness | KP | Measures whether the edited image satisfies the specified editing requirements. | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| Visual Consistency | VC | Ensures non-target elements remain unchanged and maintains consistency with the original image. | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| Image Quality | IQ | Evaluates the overall quality of the generated image. | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| Accuracy | ACC | Measures the correctness of the reasoning result for comprehension tasks. | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |

Consider adding your results to our leaderboard at Weave Project Page. Please contact weichow@qq.com for submission details!

## 📚 Citation

## About [...] `test.json`

⚠️ Note: `Image #1` references the first image, starting from 1. This represents the image index, not the conversation turn. It corresponds to the first image in the `images` array (`images`).
When using multi-turn conversations, each number index should be replaced once with `Image #{idx}<image>\n`. For single-turn, simply replace directly.

`Image #1`
`images`
`images`
`Image #{idx}<image>\n`

### ⚙️ Running Inference

The `infer/` directory contains tools for inference. To use WeaveBench, you only need to copy `loader.py` and integrate it with your model.

`infer/`
`loader.py`

For your convenience, we provide three example scenarios:

`case_step1x.py`
`case_gpt.py`
`case_bagel.py` [...] 💡 Tip: Since we require GPT to output in JSON format, some items may not be scored due to format errors. You can rerun the first script to score these items; it will automatically skip already scored content.

### 📏 Evaluation Metrics

WeaveBench evaluates 4 core metrics:

results.title: Weave
results.content: Recent advances in unified multimodal models (UMMs) have enabled impressive progress in visual comprehension and generation. However, existing datasets and benchmarks focus primarily on single-turn interactions, failing to capture the multi-turn, context-dependent nature of real-world image creation and editing. To address this gap, we present WEAVE, a new benchmark suite for interleaved cross-modality comprehension and generation. Our benchmark consists of two complementary parts. WEAVE-100k is a large-scale dataset of 102K interleaved samples spanning over 600K dialogue turns and 700K images, covering comprehension, editing, and generation tasks that require reasoning over historical context. WEAVEBench is a human-verified evaluation benchmark with 100 tasks based on 600 images, [...] evaluation benchmark with 100 tasks based on 600 images, featuring an agent-based and expert-verified evaluation framework that assesses models' abilities in multi-turn generation, visual memory, and world-knowledge reasoning across diverse domains such as science, creation, logic, and games. Experiments demonstrate that training on WEAVE-100k enables unified multimodal models to develop emergent visual-memory capabilities, while evaluations on WEAVEBench expose the persistent limitations and challenges of current approaches in multi-turn, context-aware image generation. We believe Weave provides a foundation for studying long-context reasoning and editing in interleaved vision-language models. [...] (c) WEAVEBench exhibits superior image quality. Incorporating WEAVEBench's ground truth images as in-context examples resulted in performance improvements across all models. Notably, Qwen-Image-Edit demonstrated a significant improvement of 7.1%, potentially attributable to Qwen's inherently weaker generative capabilities compared to nano-banana.
